result.summary: Analytics Hub on `analyticshub.googleapis.com` exposes a Google Cloud REST API and can also be used via the Google Cloud `gcloud` CLI; both rely on Google Cloud OAuth-based authentication.
result.description: Analytics Hub is a Google Cloud BigQuery data sharing service for publishing and subscribing to data exchanges and listings. It lets organizations share analytics assets such as datasets without copying data.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: Use Google Cloud authentication to obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token for the Analytics Hub API. The easiest path for local development is Application Default Credentials: follow [Set up Application Default Credentials](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc) or run [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login). For user-driven CLI auth you can also use [`gcloud auth login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login).
